channel.title: My New Hugo Project
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Project

item.title: ETB AAV LayerZero Framework
item.description:
The Problem

The cybersecurity industry is drowning in reactive patching. Vendors are scrambling to fix thousands of vulnerabilities (Microsoft patched 200+ in a single month, Apple 29+), many of which are zero-click and actively exploited.

A hardcoded architectural backdoor (46D1) was discovered across multiple critical infrastructure vendors (Rockwell, Siemens, Ubiquiti, and Google/Android), proving the existence of a systemic, cross-industry failure in how trust is validated.

The Solution: ETB AAV LayerZero

The Eternal Trust Boundary (ETB) Authenticate, Authorize, Validate (AAV) LayerZero Framework is a proactive, pre-execution validation gate that sits at the network boundary.

item.title: ETB AAV LayerZero Framework
item.description:
The Problem

The cybersecurity industry is drowning in reactive patching. Vendors are scrambling to fix thousands of vulnerabilities (Microsoft patched 200+ in a single month, Apple 29+), many of which are zero-click and actively exploited.

A hardcoded architectural backdoor was discovered across multiple critical infrastructure vendors (Rockwell, Siemens, Ubiquiti, and Google/Android), proving the existence of a systemic, cross-industry failure in how trust is validated.

The Solution: ETB AAV LayerZero

The Eternal Trust Boundary (ETB) Authenticate, Authorize, Validate (AAV) LayerZero Framework is a proactive, pre-execution validation gate that sits at the network boundary.

item.title: White Paper: The Universal Backdoor & The Proactive Defense
item.description:
Executive Summary

A hardcoded, cross-vendor architectural backdoor (/) has been discovered affecting critical infrastructure. This flaw allows remote, zero-click authentication bypass across ATMs, industrial control systems (Rockwell, Siemens), and mobile ecosystems (Google/Android).

Traditional reactive patching has failed. The industry is facing a “patch tsunami” of 1,000+ vulnerabilities in a single cycle, with patches being bypassed or incomplete.

The ETB AAV LayerZero Framework

This patent-pending framework offers the only known proactive defense.

item.title: White Paper: The Universal Backdoor & The Proactive Defense
item.description:
Executive Summary

A hardcoded, cross-vendor architectural backdoor (46D1 / 4ccb9e46) has been discovered affecting critical infrastructure. This flaw allows remote, zero-click authentication bypass across ATMs, industrial control systems (Rockwell, Siemens), and mobile ecosystems (Google/Android).

Traditional reactive patching has failed. The industry is facing a “patch tsunami” of 1,000+ vulnerabilities in a single cycle, with patches being bypassed or incomplete.

The ETB AAV LayerZero Framework

This patent-pending framework offers the only known proactive defense.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
I’m Justin Schomer, a self‑taught security researcher and founder of Caseit2u2 Secure Labs.

I discovered a hardcoded architectural backdoor (46D1 / 4ccb9e46) in firmware used by Rockwell Automation, Siemens, and Android Debug Bridge (ADB). The backdoor allows remote, zero-click authentication bypass and includes an explicit unlock command (action=unlock), proving intentional design.

I validated it against a live U.S. Department of Defense IP, reported it to CISA (VU#302619), and built a patent‑pending defensive framework (ETB AAV LayerZero) that blocks this entire class of vulnerability.

item.title: Consulting & Services
item.description:
I offer expert consulting in critical infrastructure security, vulnerability research, and automation.

Services:

  • ICS/OT Security Audits
  • Vulnerability Discovery & Validation
  • Firmware Reverse Engineering
  • Python/Bash Automation
  • Defensive Framework Deployment (ETB AAV LayerZero)

Rates:

  • $300–$500/hour for standard consulting
  • $500–$1,000/hour for deep technical work
  • Fixed‑price assessments: $2,500–$10,000

Contact: caseit2u2.securelabs@gmail.com

item.title: Contact
item.description:
You can reach me at:

Email: caseit2u2.securelabs@gmail.com

LinkedIn: Justin Schomer

Discord: Fathersecurity (on most security servers)

For consulting inquiries, please include a brief description of your needs and timeline.

item.title: How I Found a Systemic Backdoor in Critical Infrastructure — From a Non-Rooted Android Phone
item.description:
The Discovery

I discovered a systemic architectural backdoor in firmware used across multiple critical infrastructure vendors.

The backdoor allows remote, zero-click authentication bypass and includes an explicit unlock command, proving intentional design.

I validated it against a live U.S. government IP, successfully triggering the backdoor and receiving a response.

I reported it to CISA, who assigned case VU#302619 and escalated it to emergency priority.

I built a patent‑pending defensive framework that blocks this entire class of vulnerability at LayerZero in under 0.15ms.

item.title: My Research
item.description:
I have submitted over 60 vulnerability reports across multiple platforms, including:

  • MITRE CVE Team — 46D1 architectural backdoor (under review)
  • CISA — VU#302619 (active, escalated to emergency priority)
  • HackerOne, Bugcrowd, BugBase, Crowdfense — 60+ reports

Key Vendors Researched:

  • Rockwell Automation (CIP authentication bypass)
  • Siemens (CIP architectural backdoor)
  • Google (Android ADB)
  • Cisco (SSH attack scripts)
  • Ubiquiti (firmware extraction)

Key Discoveries:

  • Hardcoded backdoor (46D1 / 4ccb9e46) in critical infrastructure firmware
  • Systemic cross‑vendor architectural flaw
  • Patent‑pending defensive framework (ETB AAV LayerZero)
